title: Passage 1
passage: Many companies now use micro-learning programs to train employees. Instead of long classroom sessions, workers complete short lessons focused on one concept at a time. This method fits busy schedules and helps learners review topics repeatedly. However, organizations must still align these short modules with broader learning goals. Firms that implement micro-learning effectively usually track completion rates, assess real-world application, and update content regularly based on feedback.

questions.question: What is a key feature of micro-learning mentioned in the passage?
Why is micro-learning convenient for employees?
What do successful firms do with micro-learning content?
options: Update it regularly based on feedback

questions.question: The main idea of the passage is that micro-learning
options: is useful but needs structured management

title: Passage 2
passage: Urban neighborhoods are increasingly adopting community gardens. These shared spaces provide fresh produce, improve local biodiversity, and encourage residents to interact more often. Yet such projects can struggle with water access, volunteer retention, and seasonal maintenance. Municipal support programs that provide training, small grants, and compost resources often help gardens remain active and productive throughout the year.

questions.question: What is one benefit of community gardens?
Which issue can affect community garden projects?
What support helps gardens stay productive?
The passage mainly highlights
options: the role and challenges of community gardens

title: Passage 3
passage: Students preparing for competitive exams often build study routines around weekly goals. Breaking large syllabi into smaller targets makes progress measurable and reduces stress. In addition, regular self-tests help identify weak areas before final exams. Mentors advise balancing intensive study with short breaks, sleep, and revision cycles so learners can maintain concentration over long preparation periods.

questions.question: Why do students set weekly goals according to the passage?
What is the purpose of regular self-tests?
What do mentors recommend along with study time?
